reasoning: Pelvic inflammatory disease (PID) is a broad clinical syndrome caused by an ascending infection from multiple possible distinct microorganisms, primarily Chlamydia trachomatis and Neisseria gonorrhoeae. Given that it is not a single specific infectious agent but a spectrum of disease caused by various bacteria, a single "vaccine for PID" does not fit the typical biomedical paradigm. Currently, no approved vaccine exists for either Chlamydia or Gonorrhea, and though early-stage research is ongoing, neither is expected to have an approved vaccine in the near term. Even if a vaccine for one of the common causal pathogens is eventually approved, it would almost certainly be documented on Wikipedia under the specific pathogen (e.g., Chlamydia) rather than generic PID. Due to the biological reality of this multicausal syndrome and the fact that vaccine development pipelines for its principal agents are far from realization, the probability of a "PID vaccine" being developed and verified on the Wikipedia page by 2036 is extremely low. The probabilities increase slightly for the later dates to represent rising uncertainty over long forecast horizons, as per instructions.
